heaviest_rainfall.status: Record-breaking rain fell on 09 November 2025 with a total accumulation of 47.89mm over 144.8 hours and falling at a rate of 24.6mm per hour at times.

heaviest_rainrate.status: 34.3mm per hour is the maximum rain rate ever recorded on 02 September 2025, during a 15.0 hour storm which dumped 21.19mm of rain from the clouds.

longest_rain.status: The record duration for continuous rain was set on 09 November 2025, the storm lasted 144.8 hours with a total precipitation of 47.89mm and it reached 24.6mm per hour at times.

most_lightning.status: The highest recorded number of lightning strikes in a single day is {highest_count} on {highest_date} since records began on 11 April 2025.

storm.status: A storm is passing through the area.  This marks the first storm since 19 November 2025.

top_10_dry.status: The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 30 November 2024 and lasted for 10 days.

cold_snaps.status: At the moment, it is not a cold snap.  This year has seen 6 cold snaps; typically, 3.7 cold snaps happen every year.

extreme.status: The largest temperature difference recorded was 26.7°C on 29 April 2025.  On that date the temperature reached 27.1°C and fell to 0.4°C.

heatwaves.status: There is no heatwaves at the moment.  This year has experienced 6 heatwavess, with an average of 1.9 per year.

tropical.status: The last observed tropical night was 18 July 2025, making a total of only 62 times since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (201°).  In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: The top recorded wind gust is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

run.status: To date, the maximum wind run recorded is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: To date, the maximum wind speed recorded is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the inside temperature (down 4.1°C), outside temperature (down 3.5°C), and feels like temperature (down 2.8°C) and drier conditions in rainfall (down 0.1 mm).

The past week and month had observable variation in outside temperature (up 10.2°C), inside temperature (up 8.1°C), inside humidity (up 8%), and cloud levels (down 245 m) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 52.1m higher than yesterday, 244.6m higher than the past week, 274.6m higher than the past month.

The cloud levels yesterday fell between 623m minimum and 1932m maximum, with an average of 1308.1m; weekly and monthly averages were marginally higher at 1063.5m and 1033.5m.
cloud levels.summary: Clouds will be high, allowing good sunlight.

summary.summary: Inside; temperature should remain cool. Outside; extremely cold temperatures will prevail. Also, dry and less humid conditions and calm and still conditions are expected. Drizzle or light rain possible, clouds will be high, allowing good sunlight, and weak sunlight because of clouds. Weather could become unsettled.

washing.phrase: The current temperature is too low (4.6°C) for optimal drying conditions.  Any drying will take longer.
